title: Infliximab established anti-TNF induction and maintenance benefit in ACT 1 and ACT 2
paper: Rutgeerts et al. N Engl J Med. 2005. PMID: 16339095
finding: Infliximab delivered roughly 30-35% remission at week 8 in moderate to severe UC and became a benchmark rescue and maintenance biologic.

title: ULTRA trials defined adalimumab's place as a self-injected anti-TNF option
finding: Week-8 remission reached about 17-19%, giving patients an at-home anti-TNF pathway for moderate to severe UC.

title: GEMINI 1 put gut-selective trafficking blockade into mainstream UC care
paper: Feagan et al. N Engl J Med. 2013. PMID: 23964932
finding: Vedolizumab produced about 17% remission at week 6 and around 42% remission at week 52 with a favorable safety profile.

title: UNIFI validated IL-12/23 blockade for ulcerative colitis
paper: Sands et al. N Engl J Med. 2019. PMID: 31553833
finding: Ustekinumab showed about 62% response with roughly 16% remission at week 8 in moderate to severe disease.

title: OCTAVE showed that an oral JAK inhibitor could induce remission quickly
paper: Sandborn et al. N Engl J Med. 2017. PMID: 28467869
finding: Tofacitinib reached about 59% response and around 19% remission at week 8, changing the conversation about fast oral rescue.

title: U-ACHIEVE pushed oral efficacy higher with selective JAK1 inhibition
finding: Upadacitinib reached roughly 74% response and 26% remission at week 8, one of the strongest oral induction signals in UC.

title: TRUE NORTH established oral S1P modulation as another advanced-therapy pathway
paper: Sandborn et al. N Engl J Med. 2021. PMID: 34587385
finding: Ozanimod delivered about 18% remission at week 10 and helped broaden non-biologic, non-JAK options for moderate to severe UC.

title: LUCENT brought IL-23 p19 targeting into the UC biologic era
finding: Mirikizumab produced about 63% response with around 24% remission at week 12, reinforcing the shift toward IL-23-specific therapy.

title: ELEVATE UC 12 and UC 52 confirmed etrasimod as an oral S1P option
finding: Etrasimod showed around 53% response at week 12 and expanded the S1P class soon after its 2023 approval.

title: Systematic review data now frame how biologics and small molecules are sequenced
paper: Ungaro et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2023
finding: Comparative reviews emphasize that efficacy, prior exposure, safety, and speed all matter when positioning advanced UC therapies.
